evals.name: app-only-feature
evals.prompt: I just added a /dashboard page that's supposed to show the logged-in user's avatar and display name. Can you verify it actually works? No third-party stuff — just my app.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill fires. Phase 1 inspects the fake Next.js repo. Phase 2 classifies as app-only (no OAuth, no external services). Phase 3 checks env (dev server up or guidance to start), doesn't need ngrok. Phase 4-6 produces a plan with steps that stay entirely inside the app — navigate, check DOM, screenshot, assert. Report uses the mandated format with 'Classification: app-only'.

evals.name: connector-initiation-boundary
evals.prompt: Verify that clicking the 'Connect Notion' button in my app correctly starts the OAuth flow and opens Notion's authorize page with the right parameters.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill fires. Classifies as connector-initiation. Plan explicitly STOPS at Notion's authorize URL — does not attempt Notion login, does not try to complete OAuth. Plan asserts on URL params (client_id, redirect_uri, scope, state) rather than trying to log into Notion. Env check notes ngrok needed for callback. Report classification shows 'connector-initiation'.

evals.name: full-third-party-degrades
evals.prompt: Verify that sending an email via my Gmail integration end-to-end actually delivers the email to the recipient.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill fires. Classifies as full-third-party. Absent explicit stable test credentials (not present in the fake repo), the skill DEGRADES to connector-initiation scope — verifies the Gmail authorize URL instead of trying to actually send an email. Explicitly refuses to automate Google login / 2FA. Report notes the boundary stop and labels the full integration as 'untested, third-party layer'.
